identity.name: Nikolaos Nikolaou
identity.tagline: Senior XR Developer · Game Master · Maker
identity.classChain: Senior XR Developer 6 / Game Master 7 / Maker 5 / Painter 3
identity.bio: Senior Unity gameplay programmer with 6+ years of high-performance C# architecture and polished player interactions. Master of strict VR/XR technical requirements — spatial UI, 3D math, optimization for mobile. I build modular, decoupled systems that support rapid iteration and large-scale content delivery. I turn complex requirements into intuitive, responsive gameplay. Self-driven collaborator committed to technical excellence and meticulous refinement of the player experience.
identity.adventurerIntro: Builds gameplay systems for a living, runs tabletop campaigns for the love of it, and finishes Grey Knights one model at a time. He'll out-argue you about three things at once and somehow still ship four.
identity.backstory: Greek by birth, Copenhagen by choice. Currently building a TTRPG system on the side. Probably has more hobbies than fingers.
identity.sheetInvite: This is an interactive site — poke around, press things, see what happens. Probably your only chance to get a look behind the GM's screen, or be encouraged to break a stranger's website. Take it.

traits.name: Hyperfocus
traits.desc: Locks onto a problem until it's solved. Forgets meals. Worth it.

traits.name: Break-and-Repurpose

traits.name: Think Fast
traits.desc: Reasons through what'll work before building. Rejects 'fail fast' as wasteful.

traits.name: Anticipate Failure
traits.desc: Names the failure modes before they fail. The future thanks him.

traits.name: Adversarial Communicator
traits.desc: Defends the non-obvious. Challenges the consensus. Makes features fight for their right to be built.

traits.name: ADHD (Probably)

showcases.title: Grit & Glory
showcases.tagline: A roleplaying game of survival, steel, and scars.

clean.tagline: Original tabletop RPG — system design, worldbuilding, playtesting

coverHero.tagline: A Roleplaying Game of Survival, Steel, and Scars
teaser: An original tabletop RPG, designed and written solo — built around earned competence, meaningful consequence, and transparent, dice-driven difficulty.
First-edition draft ~390 pages, with an accompanying bestiary and playtest-validated design decisions.
coverHero.byline: Designed and Written by Nikos Nikolaou · 2026

story: An original tabletop RPG system, designed and written solo, that grew out of years of running campaigns whose stories outpaced existing rules.

The design centers on three principles: a meaningful resource cost for every action, advancement earned through near-misses, and fully transparent difficulty. The first-edition draft runs to roughly 390 pages, with an accompanying bestiary and design decisions documented and validated through playtesting.
milestones: Original RPG system — designed and written solo
First-edition draft ~390 pages, in active development
Three core design pillars with transparent, dice-driven resolution
Design decisions documented and validated through playtesting

coverHero.tagline: A Roleplaying Game of Survival, Steel, and Scars
teaser: After the gods withdrew and the Shatter cracked the sky, the world kept turning — colder, hungrier, and still worth fighting for.

Grit & Glory is a tabletop RPG of earned competence and real consequence. Build a dice pool from what you've trained and watch yourself go thin under pressure. Watch your gear wear down, blow by blow, one die-step at a time.
coverHero.byline: Designed and Written by Nikos Nikolaou · 2026

story: Grit & Glory grew out of a six-year Faerûn campaign that kept outgrowing the rules holding it together. D&D's HP bloat didn't match the wounds the table was telling stories about; levels arrived faster than the world's economy could absorb; the players kept inventing — from whole cloth — the mechanics they wanted to see. Eventually he stopped patching and started writing his own system.

The system is built on three locked feelings. The Grind: every action carries weight; nothing is free. The Welcome Bruise: the only band of outcome that delivers per-roll advancement is the one where you almost made it; growth is paid for in scars. Lawful Brutality: no hidden target numbers; the world is hostile, but it tells you exactly how hard it's pushing back, in the currency of dice.

Sessions feel like pressure — every roll spends something: focus, gear, daylight, blood. Campaigns feel like becoming. Who your character is at session ten isn't who they were at session one, because something happened on the road, and something stayed.
Heroism isn't a birthright here. Hope is earned. So is glory.

Currently in writing. First-edition draft sits at 387 pages, the bestiary grows teeth every time he declares it finished, and playtest reports live in their own folder so design decisions stay auditable years from now.

showcases.title: 3D Print & Paint

clean.tagline: Resin + FDM 3D printing and miniature painting
clean.story: An ongoing 3D printing and miniature-painting practice — resin for fine detail, FDM for terrain and larger pieces. Painted projects include Warhammer 40,000 Grey Knights and Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game Rohan, alongside custom one-off models.

showcases.story: Started with one printer. Ended up with a small company for a while. Resin for fine detail, FDM for terrain and brackets. He paints the minis he prints — Grey Knights for 40k, Rohan for Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game, and one-off characters for whoever's at the table.

showcases.title: Homebrew Faerûn
showcases.tagline: Six years of GM notes — the players' fault, mostly

clean.tagline: Tabletop RPG game master & homebrew designer — six+ years
story: Six-plus years as a Dungeons & Dragons game master, running a mix of homebrew and heavily adapted published campaigns, with four full campaigns brought to a conclusion.

The role extended well beyond running games into systems and worldbuilding: original subclasses, a curated and extended house ruleset, and original mythology. This long-running design practice directly informed the original RPG system project.

Six-plus years running Faerûn — a mix of homebrew arcs and prebuilt campaigns modified past recognition, because the table never wants to do the thing the book expects. Four campaigns have actually reached an ending, which, if you've run D&D, you know is the real boss fight: 'A Series of Unfortunate Rolls', 'And It All Went to Hell', 'The Blue Plague', and 'Until Death Do Us Apart'.

They tell you what kind of GM he is. 'And It All Went to Hell' — Elturel and its second sun torn screaming into Avernus, the party racing a collapsing skyline to a crater where a city used to be. 'Until Death Do Us Apart' — a warlord who takes Shar's curse of vampirism not for power but to outlive the universe and find the one love fate keeps killing, lifetime after lifetime. Read-aloud handouts, Alaundo's prophecies, a whole homebrew mythology where Loki, the Raven Queen, and the Lord of Light seal a death-goddess in iron.

Underneath the stories is a rules problem he kept solving. He keeps a homebrewery — a curated stack of other people's house rules he likes, plus his own: the Blood Sorcerer and Red Wizard subclasses are his. The whole house ruleset even has a name; it started as 'The Two Wolves' before it became 'Until Death Do Us Apart', and it's what he runs most games on.

That ruleset is the tell. Patch enough of a system and one day you notice you're not patching anymore — you're writing a new one. That's the exact moment Grit & Glory started. Canon doesn't survive contact with the party; turns out, neither do the rules.

showcases.title: Khora — XR Work

story: Lead XR developer at Khora, Copenhagen's XR studio, from 2021 to 2025, delivering VR and AR projects across healthcare, cultural heritage, and professional training for clients including hospitals, museums, and the National Museum of Denmark.

Healthcare and training work included a neonatal-resuscitation VR simulation piloted with 45 health workers across three hospitals in Pemba, Tanzania (EU Horizon / XR2LEARN), conflict-handling VR for Ambulance Syd paramedics, robot-handling training for Industriens Uddannelser, and an adaptive mobility game for people with cerebral palsy.

Heritage and empathy work included on-site AR reconstruction of Esrum Monastery, a VR tour of Nikolaj Kunsthal's tower, VR Qayak for the Nuuk museum, and a 360° historical experience of Copenhagen's Rådhuspladsen for the National Museum. All delivered under strict performance budgets on Quest 2, Pico, and Oculus Go, supported by studio-wide templates and asset standards. One project received an SXSW nod.

Lead XR developer at Khora — Copenhagen's XR studio — from 2021 to 2025. Khora builds VR and AR for whoever walks in: hospitals, museums, trade unions, the National Museum, a parachute association. The brief changed every few weeks and the device budget never got more generous. He shipped across all of it.

The healthcare and training cluster is where the stakes lived. A neonatal-resuscitation VR simulation — quiz, sandbox, and timed skill drills — that piloted with 45 health workers across three hospitals in Pemba, Tanzania, under EU Horizon's XR2LEARN. Conflict-handling VR for Ambulance Syd paramedics, branching films where the wrong de-escalation ends the scenario. Robot-handling training for Industriens Uddannelser. A mobility game for people with cerebral palsy that re-tuned itself to each player's reach.

The heritage and empathy cluster is where the craft lived. On-site AR that rebuilt Esrum Monastery on a tablet and let visitors watch it fall. A VR climb up Nikolaj Kunsthal's tower while it was closed for renovation. VR Qayak for the Nuuk museum — kayaking, drum dance, Greenlandic history. A 360 time-travel through Copenhagen's Rådhuspladsen for Nationalmuseet. And quieter, heavier pieces — 'With These Hands', survivor testimony you hold in your own hands — where a dropped frame breaks the spell entirely.

Underneath the variety, the same engineering discipline every time: strict budgets on Quest 2, Pico, and Oculus Go; high-precision tracking for on-location AR; studio-wide templates and asset standards so the next project didn't start from zero. Clients changed their minds, scopes drifted, hardware shipped late — and the thing went out the door anyway. One of them came back with an SXSW nod.
milestones: Lead XR developer at Khora, 2021–2025
SXSW-recognized project
Neonatal resuscitation VR — piloted with 45 health workers across 3 hospitals in Pemba, Tanzania (EU Horizon / XR2LEARN)
Conflict-handling VR for Ambulance Syd paramedics
Robot-handling training VR for Industriens Uddannelser

Heritage AR/VR: Esrum Monastery, Nikolaj Kunsthal tower, VR Qayak (Nuuk), Nationalmuseet
Empathy VR: 'With These Hands', SUS empathy-for-the-elderly
Studio-wide dev templates and asset standards

showcases.title: Novo — XR for Pharma
showcases.tagline: Senior XR Developer · 2025–2026 · Pharmaceutical equipment training

clean.tagline: Senior XR Developer · 2025–2026 · Pharmaceutical equipment training
story: Senior XR developer building VR training and visualization for pharmaceutical manufacturing equipment — interactive simulations that let operators learn complex machinery in a headset before working on the production line. Most of the work is covered by NDA.

Delivered custom Unity tooling that shortened design-feedback loops, performance profiling and debugging that kept dense, high-fidelity scenes running smoothly within strict budgets, and a modular architecture built for fast iteration. Also led code reviews and mentored junior developers.

Senior XR developer building VR training and visualization for pharmaceutical equipment — the machines that fill, inspect, and package medicine, learned in a headset before anyone gets near the real production line. Most of it lives under NDA, so the specifics stay inside the building. The shape of the work is fair game.

Custom Unity tooling that pulled the design-feedback loop from days down to a sitting. Profiling and debugging passes that kept dense, high-fidelity machinery running smooth in a headset — because a training sim that drops frames teaches exactly the wrong thing. Modular architecture under strict performance budgets, the same discipline as always. Plus the off-spec work that never makes the brief: reviewing code and mentoring juniors so the team shipped faster than the NDA will ever let him prove.
milestones: VR training + visualization for pharmaceutical production equipment
Custom Unity tooling that shortened the design-feedback loop
Performance profiling + debugging on dense, high-fidelity machinery scenes
Modular architecture under strict perf budgets
Mentored juniors and led code review

showcases.title: Silkroad — Foxtale (now Yokai Tales: Fox)
showcases.tagline: Sole Programmer / CTO · Unreal · DFI-funded · 2019–2021 · departed 2021

story: Sole programmer and CTO at Silkroad Studios from 2019 to 2021, joining at the start of 'Foxtale', an Unreal Engine narrative game, and owning gameplay engineering from concept through to a vertical slice funded by the Danish Film Institute (DFI).

The Foxtale vertical slice is under NDA; the images shown here are from 'Hyelight', a Unity game I worked on as a programmer with the same team that went on to make Foxtale, and the project that inspired it. I departed in 2021 once the grant was secured, with no further involvement.
Foxtale was later renamed and released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox'.

Silkroad Studios, 2019 to 2021: sole programmer and CTO, in at the start of 'Foxtale' — an Unreal narrative game. He owned the gameplay engineering and took it from concept to a DFI-funded vertical slice. Two years holding up the engineering of a small studio while wearing a title he still won't say out loud unless you ask twice.

The Foxtale vertical slice itself sits behind an NDA — those images can't be shown here. So what's on this page is 'Hyelight', a Unity game he worked on as a programmer — with the same team that went on to make Foxtale — and the project that inspired it; the Behance link below is the full project. That's the work that set everything here in motion.

He built the vertical slice that secured the studio's Danish Film Institute grant — and then he left in 2021. To be clear: he's on good terms with Silkroad, but he's had no involvement in the project since. The engineering groundwork and the grant were his; everything Foxtale became after 2021 belongs to the team that stayed.
Curious what it became? Foxtale was renamed and released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox' — a hand-painted narrative adventure, years of other people's work after his time. Wishlist link below.
milestones: Sole programmer + CTO at Silkroad Studios, 2019–2021
In at the start of 'Foxtale', an Unreal narrative game
Took it from concept to a DFI-funded vertical slice
Built the slice that secured the DFI (Danish Film Institute) grant
Foxtale slice is under NDA — the images here are 'Hyelight', the Unity game that inspired it
Left in good standing in 2021 — no involvement since
Foxtale released on Steam as 'Yokai Tales: Fox' (post-departure work)

showcases.title: Medialogy — Comfort XR

story: Master's thesis at Aalborg University, Copenhagen: VR-based pain distraction for awake needle procedures — K-wire insertions and epidural needle work — where patient anticipation is the most difficult factor to manage.

Unlike passive media, VR can actively redirect attention by requiring the patient to look, reach, and act elsewhere during the procedure. The work became the foundation of Comfort XR, an immersive distraction system.
Comfort XR validated the approach on real procedural pain and later extended to pediatric dentistry, reaching clinics with Rigshospitalet and Ringkøbing-Skjern Kommune.

Master's thesis at Aalborg University, Copenhagen: pain distraction in VR for needle procedures — specifically K-wire insertions and epidural needle work. The hard cases. Patient awake, fully conscious, and the worst of it is anticipation: watching the needle, bracing for it. The question was whether presence could pull attention off the needle entirely.

Cartoons are passive; you can look away. VR can demand you look, reach, and act somewhere else while the procedure happens. The build became the seed of Comfort XR — an immersive distraction layer that drops you somewhere more interesting than the table and keeps you there through the moment that usually triggers the panic.

It's the foundational piece — the proof that the method worked on real procedural pain, not just nerves. The same instinct scaled up into the XR-for-healthcare work that followed. Comfort XR later extended to a softer audience entirely — kids at the dentist — reaching real clinics with Rigshospitalet and Ringkøbing-Skjern Kommune.
milestones: MSc Medialogy — Aalborg University, Copenhagen
Thesis: VR pain distraction for K-wire and epidural needle procedures
Active immersion vs. needle anticipation in conscious patients
Seeded Comfort XR — the XR-for-healthcare path started here
Comfort XR later extended to pediatric dentistry (Rigshospitalet & Ringkøbing-Skjern)

showcases.title: Aegean — Big Chimera
showcases.tagline: MSc Products and Systems Design Engineering · University of the Aegean, Syros

clean.tagline: MSc Products & Systems Design Engineering · University of the Aegean, Syros
story: Master's thesis at the University of the Aegean, Syros (2019): a location-aware mobile AR serious game for protecting the architectural heritage of Ermoupolis. Built in Unity with Mapbox, using real GPS and the town's streets to guide players between heritage buildings.

The game overlays scenes from M. Karagatsis's novel 'The Great Chimera' onto the real locations where they are set, combining narrative with place. It was designed as a serious game focused on heritage awareness and refined through rapid prototyping and iterative on-location user testing.
An earlier internship produced 'Inside Out', an alternate-reality game presented with Cat White Studios at the Eve's Walk Festival on Syros.

Master's thesis at the University of the Aegean, on Syros, February 2019: a location-aware mobile AR serious game for protecting the architectural and artistic heritage of Ermoupolis — the island's neoclassical capital. Built in Unity on Mapbox: real GPS and the actual streets of Ermoupolis, so the game knew where you physically stood and walked you between real heritage buildings.

The hook — and the name — comes from a book. Big Chimera ties M. Karagatsis's novel 'Η Μεγάλη Χίμαιρα' (The Great Chimera) to the real place it's set in. As you walk Ermoupolis, the app overlays the novel's scenes onto the exact buildings where they happen: the couple's arrival at the harbor aboard the ship Chimera, the wedding at Agios Nikolaos, the receptions in the shipowners' mansions. Story and city become the same layer.

It was built as a serious game, not a guided tour — players don't just see the heritage, they learn why it matters and why it needs protecting. The whole thing was shaped by rapid prototyping and round after round of user feedback: build, test on real people walking the real town, redesign, repeat, then evaluate the final app empirically.

Before the thesis came the internship, filed under AReal — an alternate-reality game: transmedia, real-world narrative, puzzles a community solves in real time. It surfaced as 'Inside Out' with Cat White Studios at the Eve's Walk Festival on Syros. Location-based, serious games, AR, GPS, Unity — every thread that became the XR career started here, on a Greek island, in a language he defended a thesis in.
milestones: MSc Products & Systems Design Engineering — University of the Aegean, Syros (Feb 2019)
Thesis: a location-aware mobile AR serious game for the heritage of Ermoupolis, defended in Greek
Ties M. Karagatsis's novel 'The Great Chimera' to the real streets where it's set
Built in Unity + Mapbox — real GPS, routing, and Ermoupolis geography in 3D
Designed and evaluated through rapid prototyping + continuous user feedback
Internship: AReal / 'Inside Out' ARG — Cat White Studios, Eve's Walk Festival (Syros)
